DpiPromptMessage:
Windows display scaling is set to {0}%. Graveyard Keeper isn't marked high-DPI aware by default, which can make the game look blurry at 4K or other scaled resolutions.

Apply the high-DPI compatibility fix now? You'll need to restart the game afterwards.

(You can apply, undo, or silence this prompt later under the "── 9. High-DPI Fix ──" section in settings.)
DpiApplyFullSuccess:
High-DPI fix applied. Restart the game for it to take effect.

After the restart, the main menu may still look blurry on first load — open the in-game Settings → Display, change the resolution to anything else, then back to your native resolution. From that point on it renders correctly.
DpiApplyPartialSuccess:
Registry flag applied. The sidecar manifest couldn't be written (the game folder may be read-only), but the registry fix alone is usually enough. Restart the game.

After the restart, the main menu may still look blurry on first load — open the in-game Settings → Display, change the resolution to anything else, then back to your native resolution. From that point on it renders correctly.
DpiApplyFailed: Couldn't apply the fix. Check the BepInEx log for details, or follow the manual workaround from the mod's Nexus page.
DpiRemoved: High-DPI fix removed. Please restart the game for the change to take effect.
DpiWineProtonDialog: This fix only applies on real Windows. You're running under Wine or Proton, where the Windows DPI compatibility flag is ignored. Use your compositor's display scaling or Proton environment variables instead.
DpiNativeNonWindowsDialog: This fix only applies on Windows. On Linux and macOS, the operating system handles display scaling itself — there's no Windows compatibility flag to set.
DpiStatusWineProton: Not applicable — Wine/Proton ignores the Windows DPI flag. Use your compositor's display scaling or Proton env vars instead.
DpiStatusNativeNonWindows: Not applicable — this is a Windows-only fix. Your platform handles DPI scaling through the operating system.
cfg.ultrawide.name: Ultrawide
cfg.ultrawide.desc: Enable or disable ultrawide support. You must restart the game after changing this setting.
cfg.interaction_bubble_scale.name: Interaction Bubble Scale
cfg.interaction_bubble_scale.desc: Change the scale of the icons that appear above crafting stations and interactable objects.
cfg.hud_scale.name: HUD Scale
cfg.hud_scale.desc: Change the scale of the HUD.
cfg.horizontal_hud_position.name: Horizontal HUD Position
cfg.horizontal_hud_position.desc: Change the horizontal position of the HUD.
cfg.vertical_hud_position.name: Vertical HUD Position
cfg.vertical_hud_position.desc: Change the vertical position of the HUD.
cfg.zoom.name: Zoom
cfg.zoom_in.name: Zoom In
cfg.zoom_out.name: Zoom Out
cfg.remove_fog.name: Remove Fog
cfg.color_correction.name: Color Correction
cfg.borderless_windowed.name: Borderless Windowed
cfg.borderless_windowed.desc: Run the game in borderless windowed mode instead of fullscreen. Restart required.
Set Vsync Limit To Max Refresh Rate
Set Vsync limit to the maximum refresh rate of the monitor. Game default is 60fps with VSYNC enabled.
cfg.detected_display_scaling.name: Detected display scaling
cfg.detected_display_scaling.desc: How Windows is scaling your display. 100% means no fix needed; anything higher (common at 4K) benefits from the fix below.
cfg.fix_status.name: Fix status
cfg.fix_status.desc: Whether the Windows compatibility flag and sidecar manifest are currently in place for Graveyard Keeper.exe.
cfg.apply_high_dpi_fix.name: Apply high-DPI fix
cfg.apply_high_dpi_fix.desc: Tick to mark Graveyard Keeper as high-DPI aware so it renders sharply at 4K / scaled displays. Writes a Windows compatibility flag to your user registry and a sidecar manifest next to the game. Restart the game afterwards. Un-tick to remove both.
cfg.offer_the_fix_at_startup.name: Offer the fix at startup
cfg.offer_the_fix_at_startup.desc: When on, the mod will detect high-DPI scaling at startup and offer to apply the fix via a main-menu dialog. Turn off to silence the prompt (useful once you have decided either way).
cfg.check_for_updates.name: Check for Updates
cfg.check_for_updates.desc: Show a notice on the main menu when a newer version of this mod is available on NexusMods. Click the notice to open the mod's page.
